name: Pan Seared Scallops & Fennel Over Whole Wheat Pasta
recipeIngredient: 1/8 teaspoon lemon pepper, or Mrs. Dash
1/4 cup red and yellow bell pepper, julienned
1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon water
1 tablespoon fresh herbs, chopped (we used thyme)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season scallops with lemon pepper or Mrs. Dash seasoning and set aside.
2. Lightly coat a sauté pan with olive oil and place over heat. Once hot, sear the seasoned scallops on both sides until golden brown. Remove the scallops from the pan and place them in an oven preheated to 350°F (about 5 minutes) or until firm. The scallops should be cooked medium-rare for the best flavor and texture.
3. In the same sauté pan used to sear the scallops, add fennel, peppers, and garlic. Cook until the fennel becomes tender. Add Pernod and deglaze with orange juice. Squeeze the juice from half a fresh lemon into the pan.
4. Add cornstarch slurry (mixed with water) to achieve the desired consistency for a sauce.
5. Meanwhile, cook pasta in boiling water until 'al dente' (about 5 minutes), then remove and strain.
6. Arrange pasta on a plate and top with scallops and vegetables. Garnish with fresh herbs, tomatoes, and green onions.
